title: AutoVerus: Automated Proof Generation for Rust Code.
reason: LLM agent network for automated proof generation in Rust verifier Verus, integrating formal verification.

title: Large Language Model Powered Symbolic Execution.
reason: LLMs directly integrated with symbolic execution for program analysis, using path constraints and semantic reasoning.

title: Active Learning for Neurosymbolic Program Synthesis.
reason: Active learning for neurosymbolic program synthesis; handles neural mispredictions via conformal evaluation for semantic correctness.

title: Laurel: Unblocking Automated Verification with Large Language Models.
reason: LLMs generate assertions to unblock SMT-based program verifiers (Dafny), integrating formal verification with AI.

title: Memory-Safety Verification of Open Programs with Angelic Assumptions.
reason: LLM mines code patterns for formal synthesis of angelic assumptions in memory-safety verification, integrating AI with program analysis.

title: Interleaving Large Language Models for Compiler Testing.
reason: LLMs generate code for compiler testing; method focuses on semantic correctness via miscompilation bug finding.

title: Artemis: Toward Accurate Detection of Server-Side Request Forgeries through LLM-Assisted Inter-procedural Path-Sensitive Taint Analysis.
reason: Uses LLM-assisted static taint analysis for detecting SSRF vulnerabilities, integrating AI with program analysis for semantic correctness.

title: Verification of Bit-Flip Attacks against Quantized Neural Networks.
reason: Formal verification (abstract interpretation, MILP) of neural network robustness against bit-flip attacks, integrating PL/formal methods with AI model security.

title: Automated Discovery of Tactic Libraries for Interactive Theorem Proving.
reason: Automates discovery of custom tactics for ITPs (Lean, Rocq), a formal tool, using learned proof strategies.

title: What's in the Box: Ergonomic and Expressive Capture Tracking over Generic Data Structures.
reason: Presents a formal type system (capture tracking) for effect/resource safety, with mechanized proofs. LLM connection is weak, but PL/formal methods contribution is strong.

title: Multi-Language Probabilistic Programming.
reason: Multi-language PPL foundation; uses neural network for soft evidence in program analysis.
